page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server designed for high-capacity data workloads. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. A key characteristic of this server is its integrated local storage, which consists of 120,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier status in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth reaching 577.25 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, and database operations. Redis SET operations reach 214.78 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 215.95 GB/sec. However, single-core performance shows a relative tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the average tier, contrasting with the top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The CPU also supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which assist in vector and matrix calculations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7ie.metal-48xl offers high resource density by combining massive local NVMe storage with a substantial DDR5 memory footprint on a single bare-metal instance. This eliminates the need for external storage attachments for large-scale data processing. The server is suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, memory-intensive data processing, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads. The lack of a dedicated GPU makes it less suitable for GPU-accelerated deep learning, though the built-in AMX instructions support CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs via an Intel Xeon 8559C processor and 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory. It is equipped with 120,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 100 Gbps network interface. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database operations, though single-core performance remains in the average tier. The server supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. This instance is designed for high-density storage and memory-intensive workloads, making it highly suitable for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 120,000 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-throughput database and data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 1.5 TB DDR5 RAM, and 120 TB NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining massive local NVMe storage, high-bandwidth DDR5 memory, and multi-core Intel Xeon processing for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores
120,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
Top-tier multi-core performance and database operations
Best suited for large-scale databases and high-throughput data processing
